criteria_version: search/inclusion_criteria.md §1 (Stage-1 title-abstract screen) — priority batch 1, top-100 by priority score
notes: Each record individually read; reasons cite the specific design/population/outcome from the abstract. Where the population is non-commercial (e.g., social-dilemma games, cheating studies) the record is marked 'uncertain' for full-text review pending §2.1 population check.

decisions.reason: Cialdini et al. 1975: 2x2 field experiment crossing FITD/DITF × delay; self-perception test; JPSP. Eligible per §2 (compliance outcome, randomized, experimental manipulation).

2021 EJM social media influencer marketing — qualitative case-study methodology (purposeful sample of 6 SMIs, 15 videos); not randomized/experimental; need full text to confirm if any quantitative component eligible per §2.5.

EJSP 2009 gain/loss framing in health-promoting messages, 2 experiments with N=98 and N=129; mediation analysis on affect; eligible per §2 (experimental, framing manipulation, eligible behavioral-intention outcome).
EJSP 2008 N=539 randomized trial of gain/loss framing × self-efficacy in anti-smoking messages; eligible (RCT, behavior-change outcome, framing technique).
JCP 2004 three experiments extending Disrupt-Then-Reframe; explicit 'sales script' operationalization; eligible (experimental, compliance/persuasion outcome, DTR technique).
JMR 2003 two experiments on affect × framing on persuasion; mood induction × loss/gain frame; risk estimates outcome; eligible per §2.
Burger 1986 JPSP — seven experiments on TNA (that's-not-all) technique; demonstrates effect over control group; eligible (seminal TNA paper).
JMR 1983 four experiments on FITD/DITF moderation by information availability; eligible (experimental, multiple-request technique, compliance outcome).
JMR 1979 'More Evidence on Interpersonal Yielding' — field experiment comparing FITD vs DITF in commercial-request setting; eligible (field experiment, commercial context, compliance outcome).
JPSP 1979 Cann/Sherman/Elkes — three FITD experiments on blood-donor recruitment; eligible (RCTs, FITD technique, behavioral compliance outcome — donation request is per §2.4).
JPSP 1978 Cialdini/Cacioppo/Bassett/Miller — seminal lowball compliance experiments referencing auto sales practice; eligible (seminal lowball).
JPSP 1975 Cialdini et al. — three experiments on DITF reciprocal-concessions explanation; eligible (seminal DITF).
JPSP 1975 field experiment testing self-perception explanation of FITD; eligible (experimental, FITD, compliance outcome).
JPSP 1966 Freedman & Fraser — original FITD demonstration with two experiments; eligible (seminal FITD).
JPSP 1999 Davis & Knowles — four experiments establishing DTR technique with note-cards / charity-purchase outcome; eligible (seminal DTR, commercial/donation outcome).

Human Communication Research 1984 meta-analysis of FITD and DITF; eligible (meta-analysis is included per §4.5 marginal but flag — actually a quantitative review of primary studies; record for reference but primary studies it reviews are the eligible units; flag for special handling).

PSPB 1999 cross-cultural compliance experiment (Poland/US) on social-proof vs commitment-consistency, with marketing survey as target request; eligible (experimental, commercial-request context, two compliance techniques).

Journal of Communication 2025 meta-analysis of 47 gain-loss framing narrative studies (N=16,361); reports effect sizes; eligible (meta-analysis; primary references reviewed therein are eligible units).
J Health Communication 2018 RCT on gain/loss framing × communication mode for fruit/vegetable intake, N=258; eligible (RCT, framing, behavior-intention outcome).
J Health Communication 2015 three-wave experimental study on responsible drinking framing N=90; eligible (experimental, framing, behavioral-intention outcome).
Social Influence 2015 meta-analysis of low-ball compliance procedure; eligible (meta-analysis; primary references are eligible).
Communication Research 2014 2×2×3 experiment on gain/loss virtual environments; pre/post/delayed behavior outcome; eligible (experimental, framing, behavioral outcome of paper consumption).
Social Influence 2013 two experimental field studies on low-ball with deviant requests; eligible (field experiments, lowball, compliance outcome).
Psychological Science 2009 three experiments on framing × unethical decisions including negotiations; eligible (experimental, gain/loss frame, negotiation outcome).
JMR 2009 three experiments on emotion × persuasion heuristics including social proof; eligible (experimental, social-proof manipulation, persuasion outcome).
J Consumer Research 2008 four experiments on regulatory fit × self-regulation including snack choice / consumer decision; eligible (experimental, regulatory fit, consumer-decision outcome).
Journal of Communication 2008 posttest-only experiment on gain/loss antidrug ads in adolescents; eligible (experimental, framing manipulation, persuasion outcome).
J Consumer Research 2007 three experiments on DTR × need-for-closure: retail sales, willingness-to-pay, student-support; eligible (experimental, DTR, commercial outcomes).
JPSP 2004 six experiments on regulatory fit × gain-loss framing on persuasion / processing fluency; eligible.
EJSP 1987 experiment comparing FITD vs lowball for tobacco deprivation request; eligible (experimental, two techniques, behavioral compliance outcome).
Health Psychology 2020 14-week field experiment using 7 Cialdini principles on hospital visitor hand hygiene; eligible (field experiment, multiple persuasion principles, behavioral outcome).
Health Communication 2014 2×2×2×2 web-based experiment N=814 on threat/framing/choice/reactance; eligible (experimental, framing manipulation, persuasion outcome).
J Health Communication 2014 RCT N=394 on gain vs loss framing of HIV testing in Ethiopia, with rural/urban moderator; eligible (RCT, framing, intention-to-test outcome).
Health Communication 2011 experiment on framing × motivational orientation × gender for HPV vaccination intent; eligible (experimental, framing, intention outcome).
JESP 2008 cross-cultural experiment on regulatory focus × gain-loss framing in health messages; eligible (experimental, framing, intention outcome).
PSPR 1999 Burger's narrative + meta-analytic review of FITD literature identifying 6 underlying processes; eligible (review of empirical literature; primary studies cited are eligible).

JPSP 1997 three experiments on gain/loss framing in 2-person social dilemma cooperation; mechanism is framing but population is dilemma-game subjects not commercial decision-makers; need full text to judge per §2.1.
PSPB 1996 experimental investigations of framing × message processing; eligible (experimental, framing, persuasion outcome).
JPSP 1987 between-subjects experiment on framing × breast self-examination pamphlets with 4-month follow-up behavioral measure; eligible (experimental, framing, behavioral outcome).
J Applied Social Psychology 2013 three experiments extending DTR with emotional disruption; sales-request context; eligible (experimental, DTR, compliance outcome).
Health Psychology 2012 systematic review of framing × dispositional moderators; eligible (review of primary experimental literature).
J Educational Psychology Consultation 1996 randomized teacher experiment on FITD/DITF for classroom intervention acceptability; eligible (RCT, FITD/DITF manipulation, compliance outcome).
International Review Social Psychology 2017 2×3 experiment N=229 on loss-framing × issue relevance for cholesterol screening; eligible.

Online Journal Communication Media Technologies 2023 study on social-proof effectiveness for costly pro-environmental investment; eligible (commercial investment-decision outcome, social-proof technique).

J International Crisis Risk Communication 2022 2×2 between-subjects MTurk experiment on framing × imagery for charitable donation intentions; eligible (experimental, framing, donation outcome per §4.4).

JMR 2021 seven studies including Facebook Ads field experiment, eye-tracking, lab/online experiments on slow-motion video as social-proof cue; eligible (multiple experiments, advertising context, consumer-inference outcome).
Asia Pacific J Marketing Logistics 2019 two experiments on regulatory fit × goal-framing for healthy food consumer decisions; eligible (experimental, regulatory fit, consumer-decision outcome).
J Consumer Research 2008 six field/lab experiments on FITD + self-control depletion for charitable compliance; eligible (multiple experiments, FITD, donation/volunteer compliance outcome).

Communication Research Reports 2007 survey + field experiment on Legitimization of Paltry Donation × Social Proof; donation-solicitation context; eligible (experimental, social-proof, donation compliance outcome).
Communication Research 2006 two field experiments on DTR technique impact across interpersonal influence situations; eligible (field experiments, DTR, compliance outcome).
Communication Research 1994 fundraising experiment with 2×2×2 factorial testing three DITF mediation accounts; eligible (experimental, DITF, donation-compliance outcome).
Health Communication 1991 FITD/DITF experiment for health voluntarism × perceived effort/novelty; eligible (experimental, FITD/DITF, compliance outcome).
PSPB 1981 experiment(s) on consensus-information moderation of FITD with petition / shopper population; eligible (experimental, FITD, compliance outcome).
JPSP 1979 — second indexed copy of the Cann/Sherman/Elkes blood-donor FITD limiting-case experiments (deduplicate flag at extraction; same study as i=9).
JPSP 2020 direct replication of Cialdini et al. (1975) DITF across half-century time gap; eligible (replication, DITF, compliance outcome).
Int J Advertising 2015 experiment on construal level × gain/loss framing in green advertising on purchase intention; eligible (experimental, framing, purchase-intention outcome).
Journal of Communication 2012 experiment on premessage emotion × framing on persuasion; eligible.
Social Cognition 2009 negotiation experiment on regulatory fit × buyer/seller role; eligible (experimental, regulatory fit, negotiation outcome — contract value-relevant).
PSPB 1991 comparative synthesis of FITD and DITF meta-analyses; eligible (review of empirical literature).
PSPB 1986 telephone experiment N=240 on FITD/DITF × source legitimacy; eligible (experimental, FITD/DITF, compliance outcome).
J Health Communication 2019 2×2 experiment on narrative × gain-loss framing for sleep hygiene; eligible (experimental, framing, intention outcome).
Asian Journal Communication 2018 N=566 experiment on gain/loss framing × issue involvement for nuclear-energy messages; eligible (experimental, framing, message-credibility outcome).
J Health Communication 2017 N=461 2×2 experiment on framing × narrative perspective × stage of change for anti-smoking; eligible.
J Experimental Psychology Applied 2011 two-phase longitudinal experiment N=744 on framing × visual aids for STD prevention; eligible.
Health Communication 2011 experiment on gain/loss framing × perceived vaccine safety/efficacy in older adults; eligible (experimental, framing, vaccination-intent outcome).
J Consumer Research 2010 three experiments on regulatory focus × consumer choice / consideration sets; eligible (experimental, regulatory focus, consumer-decision outcome).
J Health Communication 2001 systematic review of framing × risk-information manipulations in clinical decisions; eligible (review; primary studies eligible).
PSPB 1999 two experiments testing Rothman & Salovey framing × prevention/detection behaviors; eligible.
Psychological Reports 2017 field experiment N=249 on mindfulness × FITD time-delay; eligible (field experiment, FITD, compliance outcome).
Frontiers Psychology 2017 two experiments on gain/loss × decision for self vs others; population are decision-makers but not commercial-decision context; need full text to confirm §2.1.
Management Science 2016 N=600 2×2 experiment on cheating × loss-aversion; framing manipulation but outcome is cheating not commercial compliance; need full text to judge §2.1 / §2.4 fit.
Psychological Reports 2012 two experiments on subliminal affective priming + FITD on helping/compliance; eligible (experimental, FITD, compliance outcome).
Health Psychology 2012 experiment on chronic-disease + framing on LTPA intentions; eligible (experimental, framing, behavior-intention outcome).

Psychological Reports 2008 field experiment N=360 on FITD with courtship request; eligible (field experiment, FITD, compliance outcome — courtship is interpersonal-influence per §5.5 mechanism overlap).

Psychological Reports 2002 field experiment on lowball with stranger-to-stranger dog-walking request; eligible (field experiment, lowball, compliance outcome — broadens lowball generalizability test).
Health Psychology 1995 RCT N=133 on framing × mammography utilization with behavioral follow-up; eligible (RCT, framing, behavioral outcome).
J Applied Social Psychology 1985 museum-fundraising experiment N=89 comparing FITD/DITF/lowball; eligible (experimental, three techniques, donation-compliance outcome per §5.5).

J Advertising 2021 three experiments on gain-loss framing × self/other-interest appeals for COVID mask-wearing in business signage; eligible (experimental, framing, behavioral-compliance outcome in commercial context).
Int J Advertising 2021 two experiments on social-proof (like counts) + envy in luxury social-media ads; eligible (experimental, social-proof, ad-click compliance outcome).
Basic and Applied Social Psychology 2019 two field experiments on DITF with charitable donations; dual-process explanation test; eligible (experimental, DITF, donation outcome).

Communication Research Reports 2015 three-phase experiment on loss-framing inoculation for financial-investment beliefs; eligible (experimental, framing, financial-belief outcome — financial-decision context per §2.1).
Health Communication 2013 experiment N=154 on framing × vicarious-illness experience for breast cancer screening; eligible.
Social Influence 2012 two field experiments on FITD with problematic/deviant requests; eligible (field experiments, FITD, compliance outcome).
Communication Research 2011 experiment N=128 on gender × framing for breast cancer screening; eligible.
Health Communication 2011 experiment N=1,108 on framing × time perspective for chronic-disease medication adherence; eligible (experimental, framing, adherence-compliance outcome).
J Health Communication 2010 controlled experiment on narrative × framing for exercise intentions; eligible.
J Advertising 2010 two between-subjects experiments on regulatory focus × temporal orientation × ad framing × consumer behavioral intentions; eligible.
Health Communication 2009 experiment on framing × social comparison for sunscreen behavior; eligible.
Basic and Applied Social Psychology 2003 three studies on FITD × self-concept clarity for homeless-food-drive compliance; eligible (experimental, FITD, compliance outcome).
JPSP 1995 two experiments testing self-perception mediation of FITD; eligible (experimental, FITD, compliance outcome).
Human Communication Research 1990 FITD experiments on quantity-of-behavior moderation; eligible.
JMR 1980 two experiments on DITF in business contexts; eligible (experimental, DITF, commercial-context compliance outcome).
JMR 1980 field experiment on alternative FITD manipulations in business research setting; eligible.
PSPB 1979 field experiment N=50 testing self-presentation explanation of DITF; eligible.
Psychological Reports 2005 meta-analysis (n=22 FITD vs n=22 DITF studies, subjects=1581/1611) comparing efficiency; eligible (meta-analysis).
Current Psychology 2010 experiments testing whether sequential-request techniques work via self-control depletion; eligible (experimental, FITD/DITF, compliance outcome).
J Social Psychology 1986 N=380 telephone experiment combining FITD+DITF in a hybrid sequential procedure; eligible.

J Social Psychology 1993 two perception/judgment studies on FITD/DITF × perceived power; outcome is perceived-power, not behavioral compliance — does this meet §4.4 secondary-outcome criteria? Need full text.

Social Influence 2008 experiment on FITD/DITF in virtual world (There.com avatars) × avatar race moderator; eligible (experimental, FITD/DITF, compliance outcome — virtual setting acceptable per §2.7 if behavioral analog).
Social Influence 2008 N=65 2×3 experiment on ostracism × FITD/DITF/control compliance; eligible (experimental, FITD/DITF, compliance outcome).
